# Autoshop — M1 Implementation Plan

> Synthesis of five research reports against the live scaffold ([src/recipe.rs](D:/Projects/Autoshop/src/recipe.rs), [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md), [Cargo.toml](D:/Projects/Autoshop/Cargo.toml)). Evidence tags are preserved from the source reports; `[verified: …]` = read from a primary source this research session, `[unverified]` = not yet confirmed against a real file/API. The authoritative `EditRecipe` field set is the one in `src/recipe.rs` (Report 2's schema was illustrative; Report 5 read the real one).

M1 scope (from [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:83-84](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)): RAW decode + features, unified provider framework, GPT image advisor, Claude verifier. XMP sidecar writer is M2 in the doc, but Reports 4 & 5 fully spec it and it is the primary deliverable — this plan includes a **decode-time spec lock** for it in M1 and the writer build in M2.

---

## 1. Decode stack decision: `rawloader` + `imagepipe` (primary), `rawler` (fallback)

**Decision: ship M1 on `rawloader` 0.37.1 + `imagepipe` 0.5.0, but design the decode module behind a `Decoder` trait so `rawler` 0.7.2 can be swapped in for any of the three gaps below.**

Rationale:
- `rawloader` **does decode Sony ARW** (dedicated `src/decoders/arw.rs`) and is pure-Rust → no C toolchain on Windows [verified: https://github.com/pedrocr/rawloader/tree/master/src/decoders]. It returns sensor mosaic + the calibration metadata we need (make/model, WB coeffs, black/white levels, CFA, color matrix, crops, orientation) [verified: docs.rs RawImage struct].
- `imagepipe` is the companion that turns the mosaic into demosaiced sRGB for the histogram/clipping render [verified: imagepipe depends on rawloader; `SRGBImage` = "RawImage processed into a full 8bit sRGB image with levels and gamma"].
- libraw is rejected: `libraw-rs` is **0.0.4, last published 2021-02-02**, pre-alpha, requires building/linking the C lib [verified: https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/libraw-rs]. Not worth the Windows FFI cost when a maintained pure-Rust fallback exists.

**Three known gaps in the `rawloader`+`imagepipe` stack — and the fallback that closes them:**
1. **No embedded JPEG-preview/thumbnail extraction at all** — `rawloader` exposes no preview field/method [verified: struct page lists none]. The advisor needs a preview image, so this gap is load-bearing.
2. **No full EXIF** (ISO/shutter/aperture/lens/as-shot WB) — only camera-calibration metadata [verified: struct fields].
3. **Stale camera DB** — frozen April 2022; **no A7 IV / A7R IV/V / A1 / A9 II / A6700 / A7C II** etc. (newest body is A7 III / A9, 2017–2018) [verified: directory listing has none of these].

`rawler` 0.7.2 (dnglab, published 2026-02-26) closes all three: ARW supported, `raw_metadata()` (full EXIF), `preview_image()` / `thumbnail_image()` (embedded preview), and a current camera DB [verified: https://github.com/dnglab/dnglab + docs.rs Decoder trait]. Caveat: its API is explicitly **not SemVer-stable** per dnglab README [verified] — pin the exact version.

**Practical M1 path for the preview + EXIF problem:** since `rawloader` gives neither, M1 should use **`rawler` for preview-JPEG extraction + EXIF**, and `rawloader`+`imagepipe` (or `rawler`'s `raw_image`) for the sensor/histogram render. Net: depend on `rawler` from day one for the advisor-feeding path; keep `imagepipe` only if a pure-Rust sRGB pipeline is preferred for the histogram. This is a single decisive recommendation from Report 1's own conclusion ("Use `rawler` as the primary decoder if you need ARW preview-JPEG extraction, full EXIF, or post-2018 bodies").

**Decision refinement:** Primary = **`rawler` 0.7.2** for preview + EXIF + raw image (covers all bodies, all three gaps). Keep `rawloader`+`imagepipe` available behind the same trait as the LGPL pure-Rust option for pre-2019 bodies if a `rawler` build issue arises on Windows. Pin both.

[unverified] `RawSource::new` exact constructor signature and the `image` crate re-export path in rawler 0.7.2 — API is unstable; run `cargo doc` on the pinned release before relying on exact call shapes.
[unverified] whether `libraw-rs` 0.0.4 builds against current LibRaw on Windows (not tested — and we are not using it).

---

## 2. Unified `Advisor` trait — final Rust signature + module layout

The trait must match the architecture's two roles ([docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:54-72](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)): **GPT vision → `propose`**, **Claude data-only → `verify`**. I align the method names with the ARCHITECTURE.md sketch (`propose`/`verify`) rather than Report 3's `image_to_recipe`/`verify` names, because the doc is the project contract.

**Async on tokio, `#[async_trait]` for `dyn` object-safety** [verified: reqwest async runs on tokio; async fn in traits is stable since Rust 1.75 but **not dyn-compatible** — `async_trait` desugars to `Pin<Box<dyn Future>>` to restore object safety, https://blog.rust-lang.org/2023/12/28/Rust-1.75.0/ + https://docs.rs/async-trait/]. Claude side shells out via `tokio::process::Command` [verified: docs.rs tokio process].

```rust
// src/advisor/mod.rs
use async_trait::async_trait;

/// Preview image fed to the vision advisor (embedded JPEG from the RAW).
pub struct Preview { pub bytes: Vec<u8>, pub mime: String }   // e.g. "image/jpeg"

/// Decode-time features both roles reason over (EXIF + histogram/clipping stats).
pub struct Meta {
    pub make: String,
    pub model: String,
    pub iso: Option<u32>,
    pub shutter: Option<String>,
    pub aperture: Option<f32>,
    pub lens: Option<String>,
    pub as_shot_wb_k: Option<f32>,
    pub histogram: Histogram,        // clipping %, per-channel bins
}

/// Verifier outcome — see §4 for the full type.
pub struct Verdict {
    pub decision: Decision,          // Accept | Revise | Reject
    pub reasons: Vec<String>,
    pub revised_hint: Option<String>,
}
pub enum Decision { Accept, Revise, Reject }

#[async_trait]
pub trait Advisor: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;

    /// Image role (GPT vision): preview + metadata -> EditRecipe.
    async fn propose(&self, img: &Preview, meta: &Meta)
        -> Result<crate::recipe::EditRecipe, AdvisorError>;

    /// Analyst role (Claude, data-only): acceptance-verify the recipe.
    async fn verify(&self, recipe: &crate::recipe::EditRecipe, meta: &Meta)
        -> Result<Verdict, AdvisorError>;
}
```

- `Send + Sync` supertrait so `Box<dyn Advisor>` crosses tokio worker threads [verified: tokio multi-thread runtime requires Send to move across workers].
- A provider may not support a method (Claude does no vision in M1): return `Err` rather than panic. Single-trait keeps the provider registry uniform [verified-design, Report 3 B4].

**Error type** (`thiserror` in the lib, `anyhow` only at `main`) [verified: docs.rs thiserror `#[from]`; anyhow for application top-level]:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum AdvisorError {
    #[error("http transport: {0}")]   Http(#[from] reqwest::Error),
    #[error("subprocess io: {0}")]    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
    #[error("json parse: {0}")]       Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
    #[error("claude cli exited {code:?}: {stderr}")]
                                      CliFailed { code: Option<i32>, stderr: String },
    #[error("claude reported is_error=true: {0}")]
                                      ClaudeError(String),
    #[error("provider api {status}: {body}")]
                                      Api { status: u16, body: String },
    #[error("missing config key: {0}")] MissingKey(String),
}
```

**Module layout under `src/`:**

```
src/
  main.rs                 # exists — CLI, anyhow at top level
  recipe.rs               # exists — EditRecipe contract (authoritative)
  decode/
    mod.rs                # Decoder trait: preview(), exif()->Meta, render()->histogram
    rawler.rs             # primary impl (preview + EXIF + raw image)
    rawloader.rs          # fallback impl (imagepipe sRGB; LGPL pure-Rust)
  advisor/
    mod.rs                # Advisor trait, Preview, Meta, Verdict, AdvisorError
    openai.rs             # OpenAiProvider: reqwest, Responses API + json_schema (§3)
    claude.rs             # ClaudeProvider: tokio::process claude -p (§4)
  config.rs               # serde+toml load of autoshop.local.toml; dotenvy for .env
  xmp.rs                  # M2 — sidecar writer (§5), spec'd in M1
```

**Config / keys** [verified: dotenvy loads .env; toml via serde; claude CLI needs no key]:
- `dotenvy::dotenv()` at startup; `OPENAI_API_KEY` via `std::env::var`.
- `autoshop.local.toml` (gitignored, per [ARCHITECTURE.md:74](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)) holds non-secret knobs: model ids, base URLs, claude bin path, `extra_args=["--bare"]`.
- Add to `Cargo.toml`: `tokio` (rt-multi-thread, macros, process), `reqwest` (json, rustls-tls), `async-trait`, `dotenvy`, `toml`, `base64`, plus `rawler` (pinned) and optionally `rawloader`+`imagepipe`.

---

## 3. GPT vision request — concrete shape, model, structured output

**Chosen model: `gpt-5.5`** (best reasoning, vision + structured-output capable), with `gpt-5.4` / `gpt-5.4-mini` as cheaper config-swappable alternatives (2–7× cheaper) [verified: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models + pricing table]. All three are vision-capable and support Structured Outputs ("starting with GPT-4o") [verified: structured-outputs guide].

**Chosen API + method: Responses API with `text.format` json_schema, `strict: true`.** Preview passed as a base64 data-URL `input_image` with `detail:"high"` [verified: images-vision guide for `input_image`/`image_url`/`detail`; structured-outputs guide for `text.format`].

Concrete request body (schema is the **real `EditRecipe`** from `src/recipe.rs`, not Report 2's illustrative one). Every object sets `additionalProperties:false` and every property is in `required` [verified: those three rules are required for strict mode]:

```jsonc
{
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "input": [
    { "role": "user", "content": [
      { "type": "input_text",
        "text": "You are a photo-edit advisor. Given this RAW preview and its metadata/histogram, return an EditRecipe. Stay within documented ranges." },
      { "type": "input_image",
        "image_url": "data:image/jpeg;base64,{BASE64_PREVIEW}",
        "detail": "high" }
    ]}
  ],
  "text": { "format": {
    "type": "json_schema", "name": "edit_recipe", "strict": true,
    "schema": {
      "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false,
      "required": ["version","exposure_ev","contrast","highlights","shadows",
        "whites","blacks","temperature_k","tint","vibrance","saturation",
        "clarity","dehaze","sharpening","noise_reduction","straighten_deg",
        "crop","tone_curve","rationale","confidence"],
      "properties": {
        "version":{"type":"integer"},
        "exposure_ev":{"type":"number"}, "contrast":{"type":"number"},
        "highlights":{"type":"number"}, "shadows":{"type":"number"},
        "whites":{"type":"number"}, "blacks":{"type":"number"},
        "temperature_k":{"type":["number","null"]}, "tint":{"type":"number"},
        "vibrance":{"type":"number"}, "saturation":{"type":"number"},
        "clarity":{"type":"number"}, "dehaze":{"type":"number"},
        "sharpening":{"type":"number"}, "noise_reduction":{"type":"number"},
        "straighten_deg":{"type":"number"},
        "crop":{"type":["object","null"], "additionalProperties":false,
          "required":["left","top","right","bottom"],
          "properties":{"left":{"type":"number"},"top":{"type":"number"},
            "right":{"type":"number"},"bottom":{"type":"number"}}},
        "tone_curve":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object",
          "additionalProperties":false,"required":["input","output"],
          "properties":{"input":{"type":"integer"},"output":{"type":"integer"}}}},
        "rationale":{"type":"string"}, "confidence":{"type":"number"}
      }
    }
  }}
}
```

Note `temperature_k` and `crop` are `["number","null"]` / `["object","null"]` to match the `Option<…>` fields — strict mode requires every property present, so optionals are expressed as nullable, not omitted. After parsing, call `EditRecipe::clamp()` ([src/recipe.rs:135](D:/Projects/Autoshop/src/recipe.rs)) — never trust the model's ranges.

Image-input limits (well within our single small preview): ≤512 MB payload, ≤1500 images, PNG/JPEG/WEBP/GIF [verified: images-vision guide].

[unverified] exact numeric JSON-schema size/depth/enum limits — the "Supported schemas" subsection did not render in the fetched docs; our schema is small (≤24 properties, depth 3) so almost certainly fine, but confirm at the structured-outputs "Supported schemas" anchor if the schema grows.
[unverified] exact per-model RPM/TPM (tier-dependent, not published as fixed numbers) and current gpt-4o-family prices.

---

## 4. Claude verifier — `claude -p` envelope parsing + `Verdict`

**Invocation (empirically verified, Report 3):** `claude -p --bare --model <model> --output-format json <prompt>`. `--bare` is mandatory — without it a project-level skill (`cc-enslaver`) + PROGRESS.md auto-load and pollute `.result` with a YAML block; `--bare` strips it and cut cost 16× ($0.111 → $0.0070) [verified: A1 runs]. Pass the prompt as the **final positional arg** (or via stdin) — variadic flags like `--add-dir`/`--allowedTools` swallow a trailing positional [verified: A2 failure]. The verify path is **data-only / no image**, so it needs no `--allowedTools`/`--add-dir` and runs permission-free [verified: A1].

**Envelope shape (verified raw stdout, single JSON object):**

```jsonc
{ "type":"result", "subtype":"success", "is_error":false,
  "result":"<the model's text reply>",        // <-- extract this
  "total_cost_usd":0.0070, "stop_reason":"end_turn",
  "usage":{...}, "modelUsage":{"claude-sonnet-4-6":{...}},
  "permission_denials":[], "session_id":"..." }
```

Parse plan (serde ignores unknown fields by default) [verified: serde.rs container-attrs]:

```rust
// src/advisor/claude.rs
let out = tokio::process::Command::new(&cfg.claude.bin)
    .args(["-p","--bare","--model",&cfg.claude.model,"--output-format","json", &prompt])
    .output().await?;                                   // -> AdvisorError::Io
if !out.status.success() {
    return Err(AdvisorError::CliFailed {
        code: out.status.code(),
        stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr).into_owned() });
}
#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
struct Envelope { is_error: bool, result: String, subtype: String, total_cost_usd: f64 }
let env: Envelope = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout)?;   // -> AdvisorError::Json
if env.is_error { return Err(AdvisorError::ClaudeError(env.result)); }
// env.result is the model's verdict text; ask Claude to emit verdict JSON, then parse:
let verdict: Verdict = serde_json::from_str(&env.result)?;
```

To get a structured verdict, the prompt instructs Claude to reply with **exactly** a JSON object matching `Verdict`, then we parse `env.result` as that JSON (Claude has no `response_format`; we constrain by prompt + parse + retry-on-parse-fail).

**`Verdict` type** (matches [ARCHITECTURE.md:114](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) "accept / revise / reject + reasons"):

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
pub struct Verdict {
    pub decision: Decision,
    pub reasons: Vec<String>,
    /// When decision == Revise, a short instruction for the next propose() round.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub revised_hint: Option<String>,
}
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum Decision { Accept, Revise, Reject }
```

A `Revise` (or `Reject`) verdict can trigger **one** revision round back through `propose()` with `revised_hint` injected ([ARCHITECTURE.md:114](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)).

Tooling note for the build harness [verified, not a Claude bug]: on this machine, drive Python via PowerShell, not the Bash tool (Git-Bash mis-passes args → `Non-UTF-8 code starting with '\x90'`).

---

## 5. XMP sidecar writer (M2 build, M1 spec-lock)

**Namespace:** `http://ns.adobe.com/camera-raw-settings/1.0/`, prefix `crs` [verified: developer.adobe.com/xmp/docs/XMPNamespaces/crs/]. **Sidecar naming:** `foo.ARW → foo.xmp` (replace extension, NOT `foo.ARW.xmp`) [verified: Adobe/Lightroom help corpus]. Scalars are **attributes** on `rdf:Description`; the tone curve is a **child `rdf:Seq` of `"x, y"` strings** [verified: real ACR sidecars]. `<?xpacket?>` wrapper optional [verified: KelSolaar gist].

**EditRecipe field → `crs:` key mapping** (field set from [src/recipe.rs](D:/Projects/Autoshop/src/recipe.rs); types/ranges from Reports 4 & 5):

| EditRecipe field | `crs:` key | Writable type | Conversion / caveat |
|---|---|---|---|
| `exposure_ev` | `Exposure2012` | real | identity — both stops/EV. Write decimal e.g. `"0.35"` [verified: XMP.pm; real sidecar `Exposure2012="0.00"`] |
| `contrast` | `Contrast2012` | integer | identity −100..100 [verified: XMP.pm integer] |
| `highlights` | `Highlights2012` | integer | identity [verified] |
| `shadows` | `Shadows2012` | integer | identity [verified] |
| `whites` | `Whites2012` | integer | identity [verified] |
| `blacks` | `Blacks2012` | integer | identity [verified] |
| `temperature_k` | `Temperature` | integer | Kelvin 2000..50000; valid as absolute only for RAW+Custom WB. `None` ⇒ omit key (as-shot) [verified: range Adobe/exiv2] |
| `tint` | `Tint` | integer | −150..150 in crs vs recipe's −100..100 — **scale/clamp on write** [verified: range Adobe/exiv2; recipe range src/recipe.rs:45] |
| `vibrance` | `Vibrance` | integer | identity −100..100 [verified] |
| `saturation` | `Saturation` | integer | identity −100..100 [verified] |
| `clarity` | `Clarity2012` | integer | identity −100..100 [verified] |
| `dehaze` | `Dehaze` | real | identity −100..100; absent in older sidecars — on read, score absent as neutral 0 [verified: XMP.pm real; exiftool forum] |
| `sharpening` | `Sharpness` | integer | recipe 0..150 vs crs **0..100** — **rescale/clamp on write** [verified: Sharpness range 0..100 Adobe/exiv2; recipe 0..150 src/recipe.rs:59] |
| `noise_reduction` | `LuminanceSmoothing` | integer | 0..100; **easily-missed key** — must be written or NR is silently dropped [verified: standard crs, Report 5] |
| `straighten_deg` | `CropAngle` | real | degrees; recipe = clockwise-positive — verify sign on one known image before trusting [verified key; sign [unverified]] |
| `crop.{left,top,right,bottom}` | `CropLeft/Top/Right/Bottom` | real | normalised [0,1], (0,0) top-left → identity; only applied when `HasCrop="True"` [verified: identity mapping; HasCrop gate] |
| `crop` present | `HasCrop` | boolean | `"True"` when `crop` is `Some`, else `"False"` [verified: real sidecar] |
| `tone_curve[{input,output}]` | `ToneCurvePV2012` | Seq of `"x, y"` | 0..255 ints; must start `"0, 0"`, end `"255, 255"`; ≥2 points; empty recipe curve ⇒ omit or write identity [verified: real sidecars] |
| (constant) | `ProcessVersion` | text | `"11.0"` for current PV5/2012 [verified key; "11.0" value [unverified]] |
| (constant) | `Version` | text | ACR plugin version string e.g. `"15.0"` [verified key/format] |

**Template** (`foo.xmp` next to `foo.ARW`; structure verified against real ACR sidecars):

```xml
<x:xmpmeta xmlns:x="adobe:ns:meta/" x:xmptk="Autoshop XMP">
 <rdf:RDF xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#">
  <rdf:Description rdf:about=""
    xmlns:crs="http://ns.adobe.com/camera-raw-settings/1.0/"
    crs:Version="15.0"
    crs:ProcessVersion="11.0"
    crs:Temperature="{KELVIN_2000_50000 | omit if None}"
    crs:Tint="{-150_150}"
    crs:Exposure2012="{EV e.g. +0.35}"
    crs:Contrast2012="{-100_100}"
    crs:Highlights2012="{-100_100}"
    crs:Shadows2012="{-100_100}"
    crs:Whites2012="{-100_100}"
    crs:Blacks2012="{-100_100}"
    crs:Clarity2012="{-100_100}"
    crs:Dehaze="{-100_100}"
    crs:Vibrance="{-100_100}"
    crs:Saturation="{-100_100}"
    crs:Sharpness="{0_100}"
    crs:LuminanceSmoothing="{0_100}"
    crs:HasCrop="{True|False}"
    crs:CropTop="{0.0_1.0}"
    crs:CropLeft="{0.0_1.0}"
    crs:CropBottom="{0.0_1.0}"
    crs:CropRight="{0.0_1.0}"
    crs:CropAngle="{DEGREES}">
   <crs:ToneCurvePV2012>
    <rdf:Seq>
     <rdf:li>0, 0</rdf:li>
     <rdf:li>{X_0_255}, {Y_0_255}</rdf:li>
     <rdf:li>255, 255</rdf:li>
    </rdf:Seq>
   </crs:ToneCurvePV2012>
  </rdf:Description>
 </rdf:RDF>
</x:xmpmeta>
```

[unverified] the ±100 slider bounds, crop 0.0..1.0 fraction, `ProcessVersion="11.0"`, and `CropAngle` sign — these are well-established ACR convention but were not pin-confirmed in primary docs (Adobe helpx pages timed out). Closing step: run `exiftool -G1 -s -X foo.ARW` on a raw actually edited in Lightroom, or `exiftool -list -XMP-crs:all`, and read the emitted values.

---

## 6. Eval / style harness approach (M4, designed in M1)

Ground truth = the user's **finished edits**. Two cases ([ARCHITECTURE.md:129-134](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)):

**Case A — XMP/develop settings exist (primary scorer; recipe-vs-recipe in slider space).** Read the user's `crs:` keys with one `exiftool -j -struct -G1 …` call (`-struct -j` required because `ToneCurvePV2012` is a sequence). Per image compute signed delta `Δf = recipe_f − gt_f`; aggregate **per-field MAE** (which knob the AI gets wrong) and **signed mean bias** (the tuning signal). Tone curve: sample both as a 256-entry LUT and RMSE. Crop: IoU + angle delta. **Needs only exiftool + arithmetic** — the smallest, most certain toolchain, so it is the primary scorer. Mapping caveats carried from §5: `noise_reduction`→`LuminanceSmoothing` (must add to the exiftool key list or NR goes unscored), `Dehaze` absent ⇒ score as neutral 0 not unknown, `temperature_k` vs `Temperature` is an absolute-Kelvin compare **only** for RAW+Custom WB (else use `IncrementalTemperature` as relative), `straighten_deg` sign vs `CropAngle` must be verified on one known image [verified: exiv2 crs schema; exiftool Dehaze forum].

**Case B — only an exported JPEG/TIFF (render-vs-export).** Render the AI recipe through the M2 engine, center-crop both to common aspect + downscale to 1024px long edge, then compute: **mean & 95th-pct ΔE2000** via `colour.delta_E(lab_a, lab_b, method='CIE 2000')` [verified: colour.readthedocs.io signature]; **SSIM** on luminance via `skimage.metrics.structural_similarity(..., data_range=…)` [verified: scikit-image API]; histogram correlation; estimated EV offset (least-squares gain in linear light → `log2(g)`); estimated WB offset (channel-mean ratio on near-neutral mask). Auxiliary: `exiftool` the export — many retain `crs:` tags → partial fall-back to Case A.

**Tuning signal:** convert corpus stats into **prompt deltas** (measured per-field bias lines injected into the advisor prompt, regenerated each run), a scalar objective `score = w1·MAE_A + w2·meanΔE00 + w3·(1−SSIM) + w4·(1−hist_corr)` (Case A weighted to dominate when XMP exists), and a holdout **regression gate** wired into the same accept/revise loop as §4. **Cold-start:** few-shot the advisor with 3–6 diverse (preview + the user's exiftool-read settings) exemplars before any score history exists.

[unverified] "ΔE00 < 2 = imperceptible" is convention, not a re-derived constant; the EV/WB estimators and gray-world-on-neutrals heuristic are estimates, not measurements — labeled inline so they aren't treated as ground truth.

**Dependency reality:** `exiftool` is **not on PATH**, and `rawpy`/`cv2`/`skimage`/`colour` are **not installed** [verified: `which exiftool` empty; `import` → ModuleNotFoundError]. Install before M4: `exiftool` on PATH, `pip install colour-science scikit-image numpy` (opencv optional). Case A (exiftool + arithmetic) has no Python-lib dependency → make it the default scorer.

---

## 7. Ordered M1 task list (each with an acceptance check)

1. **Pin decode deps.** Add `rawler` (exact version, e.g. `=0.7.2`) + optionally `rawloader`/`imagepipe` to `Cargo.toml`; run `cargo doc` on rawler to confirm `RawSource::new` / `image` re-export shapes.
   *Accept:* `cargo build` succeeds on Windows with no C toolchain; `cargo doc` shows the actual `RawSource`/`Decoder` signatures.
2. **`src/decode/mod.rs` — `Decoder` trait** (`preview()->Preview`, `exif()->Meta`, `histogram render`), with `rawler.rs` impl.
   *Accept:* on a real `.ARW`, `preview()` writes a viewable JPEG, `exif()` returns non-empty make/model/ISO, histogram has nonzero bins.
3. **Wire `Cargo.toml` async stack:** `tokio` (rt-multi-thread, macros, process), `reqwest` (json, rustls-tls), `async-trait`, `dotenvy`, `toml`, `base64`.
   *Accept:* `cargo build` green; a trivial `#[tokio::main]` smoke test runs.
4. **`src/config.rs` + `autoshop.local.toml`** (serde+toml; dotenvy for `OPENAI_API_KEY`); gitignore both.
   *Accept:* config loads model ids / claude bin / `extra_args`; missing `OPENAI_API_KEY` yields `AdvisorError::MissingKey`, not a panic.
5. **`src/advisor/mod.rs` — `Advisor` trait + `Preview`/`Meta`/`Verdict`/`Decision`/`AdvisorError`** (§2, §4).
   *Accept:* compiles; a no-op mock impl satisfies `Box<dyn Advisor>` and is callable across a spawned tokio task.
6. **`src/advisor/claude.rs` — `ClaudeProvider::verify`** (§4): `claude -p --bare … json`, envelope parse, `is_error` gate, parse `result` into `Verdict`.
   *Accept:* against a hand-written recipe+meta prompt, real `claude -p --bare` returns exit 0, envelope parses, and a valid `Verdict` JSON deserializes; an out-of-range recipe yields `Decision::Revise`/`Reject`.
7. **`src/advisor/openai.rs` — `OpenAiProvider::propose`** (§3): build Responses request, base64 `input_image`, `text.format` json_schema (real `EditRecipe` schema), parse, then `EditRecipe::clamp()`.
   *Accept:* against the live API with a test preview, the response deserializes straight into `EditRecipe`; `clamp()` is a no-op (model stayed in range) or pulls outliers back.
8. **End-to-end propose→verify wiring in `main.rs`** with a CLI subcommand (e.g. `advise <file.ARW>`); one Revise round re-invokes `propose` with `revised_hint`.
   *Accept:* `cargo run -- advise sample.ARW` decodes → proposes → verifies → prints an `EditRecipe` JSON + `Verdict`; a forced bad recipe triggers exactly one revision round.
9. **Lock the XMP `crs:` spec into `src/xmp.rs` as a skeleton + table-driven mapping** (§5), even though the writer body is M2.
   *Accept:* a `recipe_to_crs(&EditRecipe)` map and the template render compile; a unit test emits a well-formed XMP string for a sample recipe (validate by reading it back with `exiftool` once exiftool is installed).
10. **Resolve open question #1 (image library path)** [ARCHITECTURE.md:146](D:/Projects/Autoshop/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) — needed to point decode + eval at real files.
    *Accept:* a configured path with ≥1 real `.ARW` + ≥1 finished edit, used by tasks 2/6/7.

---

## 8. Consolidated list of EVERY remaining [unverified] item (needs a real file/API test)

| # | Item | What closes it |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `rawler` 0.7.2 `RawSource::new` constructor signature + `image` crate re-export path | `cargo doc` on the pinned rawler release; compile a real decode |
| 2 | Whether `libraw-rs` 0.0.4 builds against current LibRaw on Windows | Not needed (libraw rejected); skip unless rawler also fails |
| 3 | OpenAI structured-outputs numeric schema limits (max properties/depth/enum) | Read the "Supported schemas" anchor of the structured-outputs guide |
| 4 | Current gpt-4o-family per-model prices | OpenAI pricing page / dashboard |
| 5 | Exact per-model RPM/TPM for `gpt-5.5` (tier-dependent) | Account console / models page at our tier |
| 6 | `Exposure2012` numeric range bound (decimal type verified; ±5.0 not doc-confirmed) | `exiftool -G1 -s -X foo.ARW` on a Lightroom-edited raw |
| 7 | ±100 slider bounds for Contrast/Highlights/Shadows/Whites/Blacks/Clarity/Vibrance/Dehaze | Same exiftool read; or `exiftool -list -XMP-crs:all` |
| 8 | Crop `Top/Left/Bottom/Right` 0.0..1.0 fractional range | Same exiftool read on a cropped edit |
| 9 | `CropAngle` unit/sign vs recipe's clockwise-positive `straighten_deg` | Edit one known-tilt image in Lightroom, read `CropAngle`, compare sign |
| 10 | `ProcessVersion="11.0"` exact current value | exiftool read of a current-Lightroom sidecar |
| 11 | ΔE00 < 2 = "imperceptible" threshold (convention, not derived) | Treat as rule-of-thumb pass bar; no hard close needed |
| 12 | EV-offset, WB-offset, gray-world-on-neutrals estimators (heuristics) | Validate against a few Case-A images where true sliders are known |
| 13 | `Dehaze` presence in a given sidecar (version-dependent) | exiftool read; score absent-as-neutral-0 |
| 14 | `temperature_k` ↔ `Temperature` absolute-Kelvin validity (RAW + Custom WB only) | exiftool read of WhiteBalance + Temperature on a real edit |

Toolchain gaps that block verification of #6–#10, #13–#14: **`exiftool` is not on PATH** and **`rawpy`/`cv2`/`skimage`/`colour` are not installed** [verified this session]. Installing `exiftool` is the single highest-leverage step — it closes nearly every remaining XMP/eval `[unverified]` at once.
